title: The savings number climbing on its own
subtitle: A short visualisation for building the felt sense of savings that grow automatically.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation for when the idea of a growing savings pot still feels abstract. The scene: opening the account after a month and seeing the number higher than you left it.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

practice.whenToUse: Use to build the felt sense of savings compounding before the habit is fully established.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from the Day 38 theme. The scene is ordinary and specific: a savings account, a growing number, and your response.
Sit or lie down. Close your eyes. One slow breath.
Picture yourself at the end of next month, opening your savings account. You haven't thought about it for four weeks. The automatic transfer has run without you. You look at the number.
It is higher than when you last checked. Not dramatically: by the amount of the transfer plus any interest. But it is higher. You did nothing. It happened anyway.
Now picture the month after that. And the one after that. Each time you look, the number is a little higher. You are not fighting for it. It is building on its own.
Hold that picture for two minutes. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.
